compositional_truth_check.findings: P3 (Pothos) is present with the anchor. P1 (String of Nickels) is present in the yellow pot. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are absent or in a state of total systemic loss, replaced by a single, struggling sprout in the black pot.

inventory_reconciliation.new_introduction: Unidentified seedling/sprout in the black pot (P2/P4 location).

plant_audit.p1_string_of_nickels: Stable, low density, minimal growth observed.
plant_audit.p3_pothos: Significant chlorosis and necrosis on the lower leaf; structural integrity is failing.
plant_audit.p2_p4_black_pot: Systemic loss of original specimens. One small, uncatalogued sprout is present.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dry and compacted across all pots.
biome_observations.surface_debris: White perlite/grit present; no fungal blooms detected.

temporal_deltas.t_minus_5_to_current: The Pothos (P3) leaf has shown progressive yellowing and browning (necrosis) at the tip. The black pot (P2/P4) has remained largely barren, save for the emergence of a single, small sprout that has not significantly developed over the 5-day window.

vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of decline. The Pothos (P3) is exhibiting clear signs of physiological stress, likely due to nutrient deficiency or improper moisture levels, evidenced by the spreading necrosis on the primary leaf. The P2/P4 pot has suffered a total loss of the registered species, with only a singular, unidentifiable sprout remaining. The environment is stable but the biological occupants are struggling to maintain health.
